skill.name: pydicom
skill.description: A Python library for working with DICOM (Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine) files. Use this skill when you need to read, write, or modify DICOM format medical imaging data, extract pixel data from medical images (CT, MRI, X-ray, ultrasound), anonymize DICOM files, process DICOM metadata and tags, convert DICOM images to other formats, handle compressed DICOM data, or work with medical imaging datasets. Suitable for tasks involving medical image analysis, PACS systems, radiology workflows, and healthcare imaging applications.
skill.summary: Read, write, and process DICOM medical imaging files with Python. Extract metadata, anonymize patient data, convert images, and work with compression formats using the pydicom library.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 269 potential issues with a risk score of 100/100, but all findings are false positives after semantic evaluation. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings (153 locations) are markdown code fence backticks in documentation files, not actual shell execution. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false matches on DICOM tag hex values (e.g. 0x0010,0x0010) and attribute name strings. The 'Hardcoded IP address' findings are DICOM Transfer Syntax UIDs (e.g. 1.2.840.10008.1.2). Network references are all legitimate documentation URLs. This is a legitimate medical imaging skill with standard Python scripts for DICOM processing. Risk level is low because the skill runs Python scripts and writes files, which is expected behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby/shell backtick execution in documentation files
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 153 instances of backtick usage across SKILL.md, references/common_tags.md, and references/transfer_syntaxes.md as Ruby shell execution. All are markdown code fence backticks used to delimit Python and bash code examples. These are documentation files, not executable scripts. No actual shell command injection risk exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm on DICOM tag hex values
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged DICOM tag hex identifiers (e.g. 0x0010, 0x0010 for PatientName) and DICOM attribute name strings as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are standard DICOM tag identifiers and Python attribute names used for medical imaging data access. No cryptographic operations exist in this codebase.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded IP address matching DICOM UID constants
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged DICOM Transfer Syntax UID strings (e.g. 1.2.840.10008.1.2) as hardcoded IP addresses. These are standard DICOM unique identifier strings that identify encoding formats like Implicit VR Little Endian. They are not network addresses and pose no security risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs as documentation references
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 15 hardcoded URLs across SKILL.md, references/common_tags.md, and references/transfer_syntaxes.md. All URLs point to official documentation: pydicom documentation, DICOM standard, and related reference sites. These are standard attribution links and pose no exfiltration risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Heuristic risk combination warning
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a heuristic 'dangerous combination' of code execution, network, filesystem, and credential access across multiple files. Evaluation confirms all risk factors stem from legitimate uses: markdown code examples (execution), documentation URLs (network), user-specified file output (filesystem), and DICOM PHI tag handling (credentials). No malicious combination pattern exists.

content.user_title: Process medical DICOM images with pydicom
content.value_statement: Medical imaging workflows require specialized tools for DICOM format files. This skill provides Python scripts and guidance for reading, anonymizing, converting, and extracting metadata from CT, MRI, X-ray, and ultrasound DICOM files.
actual_capabilities: Read DICOM files and access patient, study, and image metadata
Anonymize DICOM files by removing Protected Health Information (PHI)
Convert DICOM pixel data to standard image formats (PNG, JPEG, TIFF)
Extract and display DICOM metadata in text or JSON format
Provide code examples for pixel data extraction, windowing, and compression handling
Reference comprehensive DICOM tag and transfer syntax documentation
limitations: Requires pydicom and numpy to be installed in the Python environment
Compressed DICOM formats need additional handler packages (pylibjpeg, python-gdcm)
Scripts process one file at a time; batch processing requires custom looping logic
DICOM-to-image conversion produces 2D representations; 3D volume reconstruction is not built in

use_cases.title: Radiology researcher processing study data
use_cases.description: Extract metadata and convert DICOM images from a research study into PNG format for analysis and publication. Use the anonymize script to remove patient identifiers before sharing with collaborators.
use_cases.target_user: Medical researcher working with imaging datasets

use_cases.title: Healthcare developer integrating PACS data
use_cases.description: Build pipelines that read DICOM files from PACS systems, extract pixel data and metadata, and feed processed images into machine learning models or clinical decision support tools.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineer in medical imaging or health IT

use_cases.title: Clinical data analyst preparing imaging reports
use_cases.description: Automate extraction of DICOM metadata fields (modality, study date, patient demographics) into structured reports. Validate image properties like pixel spacing and slice thickness for quality control.
use_cases.target_user: Data analyst in a hospital or clinical research setting

prompt_templates.title: Read and display DICOM metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Read a DICOM file from {input_path} and display the patient name, study date, modality, and image dimensions. Use the extract_metadata.py script or pydicom library directly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick inspection of a single DICOM file

prompt_templates.title: Anonymize a DICOM file for sharing
prompt_templates.prompt: Anonymize the DICOM file at {input_path} and save the result to {output_path}. Replace PatientName with ANONYMOUS and PatientID with ANON001. Show which fields were modified.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing medical data for external collaboration

prompt_templates.title: Convert DICOM to image format
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the DICOM file at {input_path} to a PNG image saved at {output_path}. Apply windowing if available. Report the image dimensions and modality from the source DICOM file.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating visual representations from medical scans

prompt_templates.title: Batch process DICOM series into 3D volume
prompt_templates.prompt: Read all DICOM files from the directory {dicom_dir}. Sort them by InstanceNumber or ImagePositionPatient. Stack the pixel arrays into a 3D numpy volume and report the shape and voxel spacing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building volumetric data from a CT or MRI series

output_examples.input: Anonymize a CT scan DICOM file containing patient identifying information
Convert chest X-ray DICOM to viewable PNG with windowing applied
Extract DICOM metadata as structured JSON
output: Key fields in the output include patient information
and image properties (Modality: CT, Rows: 512, Columns: 512, PixelSpacing: 0.5\0.5).

best_practices: Always check for missing DICOM attributes using hasattr() or get() before accessing them. DICOM files may lack optional tags, and direct access can raise AttributeError.
Use apply_voi_lut() for proper medical image display windowing. Raw pixel values from CT and MRI scans require window center and width adjustments for correct visualization.
Always validate DICOM anonymization output by reviewing which fields were modified. Use the verbose flag to confirm PHI tags are properly removed or replaced before sharing data.
anti_patterns: Do not access DICOM attributes like ds.PatientName without checking if the tag exists first. Files from different modalities and vendors have different required tags, and missing attributes will cause errors.
Do not skip installation of compression handlers when processing compressed DICOM files. Without pylibjpeg or gdcm, pixel data decompression will fail and the error message may be unclear.
Do not modify DICOM files and save them without preserving the original transfer syntax. Using write_like_original=True ensures compatibility with the target system or PACS.

faq.question: What is pydicom and what does this skill do?
faq.answer: Pydicom is a pure Python library for reading, writing, and modifying DICOM medical imaging files. This skill provides Python scripts and documentation for common tasks like metadata extraction, anonymization, and image format conversion.

faq.question: Do I need to install any packages to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need pydicom and numpy installed. For image conversion you also need pillow. For compressed DICOM files, additional handlers like pylibjpeg or python-gdcm may be required.

faq.question: Can this skill handle all DICOM compression formats?
faq.answer: It supports common formats if the required handler packages are installed. Uncompressed formats work without extra dependencies. JPEG, JPEG 2000, JPEG-LS, and RLE are supported with appropriate handlers.

faq.question: Does the anonymization script fully remove patient data?
faq.answer: The script removes or replaces 36 common PHI fields including name, ID, birth date, address, and physician names. It does not anonymize UIDs by default. Review the output to confirm all sensitive data is handled.

faq.question: Can I process multiple DICOM files at once?
faq.answer: The scripts process one file at a time. For batch processing, use Python to loop over files in a directory and call the script functions programmatically or with a shell loop.

faq.question: What image formats can I convert DICOM files to?
faq.answer: The dicom_to_image.py script supports PNG, JPEG, TIFF, and BMP formats. You can optionally apply windowing for correct medical image brightness and contrast.
